moh0azl.text: Good one! The adress is processed in the end and then it's correct. We successfully send some letters to the UK now. But might be weird now that the preview is not correct. Add it to my TODO's. Thanks for the feedback.
moh0azl.parent_text: Your addresses in the UK are putting the number at the end of the street name which is not typical for letters here, it should be at the start.

moka5t8.text: whoops, I meant on the map page. That was the first page I was sent to from this thread, and on it, there's only a hyperlink I guess as a consumer, I assumed there'll be different costs for a letter in the US than, say, New Zealand. But if its a flat fee anywhere in the world that's great!
moka5t8.parent_text: Hi there! Thank you so much! - You mean the hyperlink on the around the world page? On the homepage there is a proper button? - Costs are calculated before you enter your creditcard details. Above you see total costs?

momtpzn.text: Good to know! What made it unclear? :) Hmm good one, not sure FransBauer hehe.
momtpzn.parent_text: First of all great idea! I saw some Instagram advertisements of this service, but that didn't make it clear for me what this service was for. Until this post ;) But isnt 'Pieter Post' a copyrighted name, or a name that can get you into some sort of legal trouble when using commercially?  Just a heads up, since this is also used for the famous children cartoon (Dutch version of Postman Pat):))

mohjaik.text: The price isn't high though, I was scoffing preemptively but was glad to see €2.
mohjaik.parent_text: Please include pricing on the first page. I don't want to write a long letter only to see a high price at the end.

mpd184c.text: I don't even know where it is on my keyboard! — No just kidding, always used and loved the —. Sad it's now a CHATGPT thing, better to not use it for a while.
mpd184c.parent_text: I like the idea, super cool! It would be great if people could write a simple text in their own handwriting style, so you could parse it and use it for their physical mail. I think that would give it a more personalized and less sterile touch. Unrelated, out of curiosity, did you ask ChatGPT to write or proofread the description of your post? Nothing against it, I do it too. I just want to confirm my theory that when you see a message with a '—', it's ChatGPT 99% of the time.
